system.effect: The character has accumulated Insanity Points from mental trauma. At certain thresholds, they must roll on the Shock Table or Trauma Table, potentially gaining disorders.
system.removal: Insanity Points are permanent and cannot be easily removed. Some rare treatments or extended downtime may reduce them at the GM's discretion.

description.value:
The character's mind has been scarred by exposure to the horrors of the galaxy. Insanity is tracked as a point total. At certain thresholds, the character's mental state degrades:

  • 0–29 Insanity Points: No significant effect.
  • 30–59 Insanity Points: The character gains a Minor Disorder.
  • 60–89 Insanity Points: The character gains a Severe Disorder.
  • 90–99 Insanity Points: The character gains an Acute Disorder and is barely functional.
  • 100+ Insanity Points: The character is irrevocably insane and becomes an NPC.

system.notes: Track total Insanity Points via stacks. Disorders are applied separately as needed.
